title: Discussion: SVA support roadmap — alignment and contribution plan
body:
Hi, @wsnyder 

With randomization support maturing, we (PlanV) are looking to move on to SVA as the next focus area alongside coverage part (waiting for the baseline PR #7117  done).

Looking at the current state, I see that §16.7/16.9/16.10 are listed as Unsupported in #6941, and #4425 tracks the main sequence support gap.

I've been working locally on a few initial fixes — named sequence references, ##N in implication RHS, expression-level or/and, and property-local variables.
These are small changes (each 10–75 lines in src/) and I'll be opening PRs for them soon.

Beyond these, there's quite a bit more to cover: range delays ##[M:N], repetition [*N], temporal operators (nexttime/always/eventually/until), etc.

Would it be helpful to have an SVA detailed roadmap similar to what we've been planning to do for coverage? I have a rough draft that breaks things into phases by complexity. Happy to refine and share it here if that's useful.

Any thoughts on plans, priorities, or suggestions for this area would be highly appreciated!

Thanks!
